name: FLORENTINE GROTESCHI #12
description: Florentine Groteschi #12 is a papal terminal single-panel relic built around Medici and Flash Game Guildhall. Tarocchi Fiorentini assigns Sei di Bastoni, used here as the witness card. A Malachim margin note under a Solomonic seal gets pulled into a zodiac-risk dashboard for patron liquidity, making the panel read like a corrupted receipt for belief, status, and patronage. Cosimo approves the lighting and ignores the crime. The generated insert, a mutant laurel wreath made of browser tabs, acts as the modern clue; Gematria marks GROTESCHI at Σ 624.

attributes.value: a mutant laurel wreath made of browser tabs

florentine_groteschi.collector_note: Custom top-level lore data: not an OpenSea trait list, but intentionally readable in the raw token JSON.

chess_piece.name: Pawn

rarity.ranking_rule: Higher chess strength/rank is intentionally rarer: Pawn common, Knight/Bishop uncommon, Rook rare, Queen epic, King legendary.

board_position.position_rule: Deterministic per-token board square reserved for future game/use mechanics; independent of visible glyph placement.

chess_piece.allocation_rule: Weighted deterministic shuffled chess allocation by piece strength: {'Pawn': 560, 'Knight': 280, 'Bishop': 280, 'Rook': 168, 'Queen': 84, 'King': 29}
chess_piece.metadata_visibility: Hidden from OpenSea attributes; stored only in florentine_groteschi.hidden_traits.
chess_piece.visual_rule: A small unlabeled foreground chess glyph shows the chess-piece graphic with the piece name encoded underneath; that visible code is intentionally not serialized in metadata.

visible_text.history_now_bridge: MEDICI RECEIPT DRAMA: a Malachim margin note under a Solomonic seal gets pulled into a zodiac-risk dashboard for patron liquidity. Sei di Bastoni reads the jump as patronage theater: who certifies the relic, who pays for belief, and who gets the receipt. Cosimo approves the lighting and ignores the crime.

artwork_breakdown.generated_insert: a mutant laurel wreath made of browser tabs

tarocchi_fiorentini.allocation_rule: 14 complete 97-card sets across 1,401 tokens, plus 43 deterministic extras

artwork_breakdown.composition_read: Medici anchors the historical source. Sei di Bastoni supplies the witness logic. The mutant laurel wreath made of browser tabs acts as the modern meme-object. The papal terminal palette and Flash Game Guildhall styling make the panel read like a corrupted Renaissance case file.

groteschi_explanation.plain_english: Read plainly: Medici is treated as evidence in a medici receipt drama. The old-world clue is a Malachim margin note under a Solomonic seal; the modern-world echo is a zodiac-risk dashboard for patron liquidity. The joke is that patronage, holy paperwork, and marketplace verification are all status systems asking who gets believed.
groteschi_explanation.read_between_the_lines: The Sei di Bastoni card is not decorative; it tells you how to read the room. The old clue and the modern clue compete as evidence, so the panel becomes a custody argument about belief, status, and receipts. Cosimo approves the lighting and ignores the crime. The slang turns sacred authority into aura, patronage into clout economics, and marginalia into groupchat evidence.

slang_for_normal_plebs.translation_for_normal_plebs: did it perfectly; in this collection a fresco, saint, or relic can 'eat' like a viral post
someone taking a cut; mapped to patronage fees, guild dues, and marketplace rake
charisma or persuasive charm; patronage influence rephrased as social magic
self-mythologized lone-wolf status; a patron, saint, or relic trying too hard to seem untouchable
confused disbelief in groteschi dialect; a marginalia scream when the symbols overperform

hidden_solution.method: Split the visible marker into DNA triplets, translate each triplet through the genetic-code table, then read the amino-acid letters as the source Base name. Non-standard letters use documented stop/nearest triplet placeholders.

hidden_acrostic.method: Read the first character of each acrostic line; slashes are spaces.

collector_warnings: If the Tarocchi card and QR code disagree, trust the card.
Do not cite the notary after midnight.
If the ledger balances, something has gone terribly wrong.
The archive denies all aura liability.
Do not let the codon marker breed near the provenance strip.
